objective: Implement PU-012 by selecting and proving a Codex runtime evidence producer bridge that admits observable Codex runtime facts into Coding Harness without scraping final assistant prose or mutating the Codex checkout.

bridgeDecision.description: The first producer bridge is a Harness-owned wrapper/import boundary that validates explicit Codex runtime evidence packets or structured exported facts. It must preserve source provenance and classify unsupported fields as unknown rather than inferring them.

rejectedBoundaries.reason: Inspected SDK event types expose thread.started thread_id, but turn.started is currently only a type marker and does not supply the turn_id, permission profile, or trace facts required for claim-supporting evidence.
Mutating /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex requires a separate Codex-owned contract, rollback path, and validation gate. PU-012 may inspect Codex source as evidence, but must not patch it in this slice.
The verifier cockpit is explicitly designed to avoid treating final assistant prose as runtime truth.

sourceSnapshot.freshnessPolicy: implementation is blocked if repoHeadSha or any source blob sha differs before PU-012 source-provenance tests are updated and this intent is re-reviewed

sourceEvidence.observed: ThreadStartedEvent carries thread_id; TurnStartedEvent does not expose a turn id in the inspected type surface.
Thread execution setup includes model, sandboxMode, workingDirectory, networkAccessEnabled, webSearch settings, approvalPolicy, and additionalDirectories.
Analytics facts include thread_id, turn_id, model, permission_profile, approval_policy, approvals_reviewer, sandbox network access, and token usage fields.
JSON-RPC request shape includes optional W3C trace context; trace availability is source-dependent and must remain unknown unless attached by the active producer.

outOfScope: SDK package publication or generated app-server protocol changes
src/commands/runtime-card.ts, src/commands/next*.ts, or public cockpit command changes reserved for PU-013
review-state, external-state, delivery-truth, or pr-closeout semantic changes reserved for PU-014 and later
scraping assistant final responses, raw transcripts, prompts, secrets, credential files, or bulky telemetry
claiming full lifecycle implementation or PR merge readiness

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: intent artifact is syntactically valid JSON before review

automationPlan.name: Codex source snapshot freshness check
proves: pinned Codex source observations are revalidated against commit and blob provenance before implementation starts

automationPlan.name: shared runtime contract untouched check
proves: PU-012 did not silently mutate the shared codex-runtime-evidence or runtime-evidence-adapter contracts reserved for earlier and later lifecycle units

automationPlan.name: Codex producer bridge tests
proves: producer bridge emits or imports only validated Codex runtime evidence and preserves existing adapter behavior

automationPlan.name: Codex source provenance tests
proves: source paths, source kinds, confidence, unsupported fields, and failureClass values are machine-checked

automationPlan.name: focused runtime formatting
proves: changed runtime deep modules satisfy formatting and lint checks

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: goal state and receipts remain valid after PU-012 updates

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, ownedAcceptanceIds, bridgeDecision, sourceSnapshot, sourceEvidence, inScope, outOfScope, guardedPathGlobs, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending

parallelPrTriageStrategy.parallelCodeWorkAllowed: only after this PU-012 intent reaches reviewed status and branch ownership remains with the coordinator
blockedWhen: PR #305 remote checks fail on the active branch
a reviewer requests mutation to this intent before implementation starts
parallelPrTriageStrategy.safeFallback: continue source discovery, reviewer artifact verification, and local intent refinement without touching production runtime code

stopConditions: Stop if the adversarial or agent-native reviewer identifies a material bridge-boundary issue and the intent has not been patched and re-reviewed.
Stop if implementation would require mutating /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex before a separate Codex-owned spec, rollback path, and validation gate exist.
Stop if /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex sourceSnapshot repoHeadSha or any sourceEvidence gitBlobSha differs before this intent and source-provenance tests are updated and re-reviewed.

Stop if implementation requires editing src/lib/runtime/codex-runtime-evidence.ts or src/lib/runtime/runtime-evidence-adapter.ts; create a separate additive contract intent addendum before touching shared runtime contracts.
Stop if the producer cannot distinguish claim-supporting evidence from orientation-only source observations.
Stop if unsupported Codex fields would be inferred instead of represented as unknown with failureClass.
Stop if the new producer path would broaden runtime-card, harness next, delivery-truth, review-state, external-state, or pr-closeout behavior outside PU-012.
rollback: Retain codex-runtime-evidence/v1 schema and runtime-evidence-adapter validation unless the producer change required an invalid additive extension.
Restore this intent to pending if review evidence is invalidated.
Do not modify or roll back /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex because this slice must not mutate it.
assumptions: The Harness-owned wrapper/import bridge is sufficient for PU-012 because it can prove the producer boundary without upstream Codex mutation.

Source observations from /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex are pinned to the recorded repoHeadSha and gitBlobSha values; if those change, this intent returns to pending review before implementation proceeds.
PR #305 can remain draft while PU-012 intent review proceeds, but no merge-readiness claim is allowed from draft state.
